compiled_state.title: rebar reopen with no args crashes with raw bash 'unbound variable' instead of usage
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
rebar reopen with no arguments crashes with a raw bash 'unbound variable' diagnostic instead of usage text.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. rebar reopen
2. Output: .../rebar: line 291: $1: unbound variable (exit 1). Compare: rebar claim / rebar archive with no args print proper Usage text.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: misuse prints the reopen usage line to stderr, exit 1, no shell diagnostics (the sibling-command contract).
- Actual: raw set -u failure from the dispatcher.

## Source
src/rebar/_engine/rebar:291 — reopen arm dereferences "$1" after _strip_output_flags/set -- with no arity check, under set -u.

## Fix direction
Arity guard before the dispatch: [ $# -ge 1 ] || { _print_subcommand_help reopen >&2; exit 1; } (match the pattern used by sibling arms). Validated diff in the implementation-notes comment.

## Suggested RED test
rebar reopen (no args) -> exit 1, stderr contains "Usage: rebar reopen", stderr does NOT contain "unbound variable"; rebar reopen <closed-id> still reopens.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No-arg reopen prints usage, exit 1, zero bash diagnostics (RED test first)
- [ ] Happy-path reopen unchanged
- [ ] Audit sibling arms for the same hole; fix any found in the same pass

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09): guard must go AFTER _strip_output_flags / set -- so `rebar reopen --output json` (flags only, no id) is also caught. Validated diff in the reopen arm (src/rebar/_engine/rebar, after set -- line ~290):
   if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
       _print_subcommand_help reopen >&2
       exit 1
   fi
Validated: no-arg reopen prints the Usage line to stderr, exit 1, zero bash diagnostics; create->close->reopen <id> still works. NOTE: grep shows NO existing test mentions reopen — the RED test is net-new coverage (suggested home: a reopen section in a new or existing dispatcher test).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

verify_commands.command: bash -c 'out=$(rebar reopen 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q "Usage: rebar reopen" && ! echo "$out" | grep -q "unbound variable" && [ $rc -eq 1 ] && echo PASS'
verify_commands.dd_text: no-arg reopen prints usage, no bash diagnostics
